preamble: The shift from the palatal nasal [ɲ] to the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] represents a significant weakening (lenition) where the nasal closure is lost, and the airflow becomes turbulent. This process often occurs in environments favoring the loss of nasality in favor of increased frication.

examples.note: Dialectal weakening of palatal nasal in specific rural or rapid speech contexts.
Northern Portuguese dialectal variants showing palatal nasal fricativization.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
